rationale_en: A routine annual budget is a small force on aggregate GDP but a direct input to that year's government consumption expenditure, so the bundle nets out to a partial, real effect.
This is the FY1404 budget law itself and the Annual Budget Law Totals chart exists specifically to record the totals it sets.
One year's budget is a small force against GDP, inflation, investment and trade, each driven mainly by oil prices, sanctions and exchange rates.
Budget laws reference an official rate but do not set the market dollar rate, so the expected effect on this series is marginal.
The ruling directly restores the statutory export tax exemption, a major and legally direct instrument for this narrow tax-on-exports series.
Removing one unlawful export levy is a marginal input to the broad international-trade-tax base and merchandise export volumes.
It falls in the chart's time window but is the base budget bill, not itself a mid-year supplementary addition, so its expected effect on this specific series is partial.
A routine annual budget bill amid revolutionary transition, one of many instruments in the Government Finance category.
The guaranteed-purchase-price mechanism this law creates is the direct policy input that sets the wheat procurement and producer price series.
Barley and rice have only intermittently fallen under the guaranteed-purchase scheme, so the expected effect on their prices is real but marginal.
A diffuse framework-level claim over the whole agriculture-prices category with no specific crop channel named.
Free-zone tax and customs incentives are a real but partial driver of FDI and new business formation, one force among many national and global factors.
Free zones are a small share of total national export volume, so the expected effect on economy-wide export series is marginal.
A 1943 budget act predating every chart series currently indexed, included for historical domain coverage only.
A routine annual budget law, one of many such instruments in the Government Finance category.
A direct sectoral instrument setting production quotas, purchase arrangements and import controls specifically for tea, a major lever on this exact commodity's series.
An early framework of tax and customs incentives for exporters, but a small force against export series dominated by oil-era macro conditions.
The production-promotion side of the law bears only very indirectly on industrial value added.
ECOTA is a real named preferential-tariff channel but a small share of Iran's total trade with the MENA region.
The core statutory framework governing all licensing, permitted goods and customs procedure for Iran's entire merchandise trade regime.
A 1935 supplementary budget act, included for legislative-history completeness and predating all chart series in the index.
This budget falls within the chart's exact window and is itself one of the allocation channels the oil-revenue-by-allocation series tracks.
A routine annual budget law, one of many instruments in the Government Finance category.
The core statutory trade framework of the Iran-Iraq war economy, a major direct instrument shaping merchandise trade in this period.
A routine annual budget law, one of many instruments in the Government Finance category.
A dedicated foreign-exchange budget allocating hard currency across multiple official rates, a major direct instrument behind the official-versus-parallel dollar rate gap in this period.
A specialized foreign-exchange budget is tangential background to the general Government Finance category.
Codifies recurring development-plan provisions into permanent statute, a broad but indirect legal-recodification with real partial effect across many macro channels.
A narrow 2016 housing-finance bylaw that barely overlaps the charts' historical windows (ending 2006 and 2016), a marginal channel to housing stock.
A routine annual budget law, one of many instruments in the Government Finance category.
One year's budget is a small force against aggregate GDP, dwarfed by oil revenue and reconstruction-era macro conditions.
A 1971 institutional founding act for a predecessor company; the modern caviar and sturgeon charts track a different successor entity decades later, so no direct expected channel remains.
Background institutional context for the fisheries sector broadly, not a direct policy lever on output.
A narrow amendment to a credit fund's founding statute, a diffuse claim on the whole agriculture-production category.
A state agricultural-credit fund is not part of private-sector credit, so there is no real channel to this series.
A headline nationalization of large industrial firms, a real but partial force on aggregate GDP alongside the far larger effects of revolution and war.
The law directly transferred ownership and management of Iran's largest industrial and manufacturing firms to the state, structurally reorganizing what these value-added series measure.
Reshaping ownership of major industrial assets had a real but indirect effect on the investment climate behind fixed capital formation.
An export-credit-insurance vehicle is a narrow instrument with negligible expected effect at the broad Trade (Exports) category level.
A background credit-insurance institution, marginal against total exports of goods and services.
This FY1372 budget law is a direct enacted data point within the exact span the Annual Budget Law Totals chart tracks.
Coincided with the 1993 official exchange-rate unification, a real but partial force since that FX policy was a separate decision from the budget law itself.
One year's budget is a small force on GDP compared to oil prices and the unfolding external-debt and FX crisis.
A routine wartime annual budget law, one of many instruments in the Government Finance category.
A wartime budget is one force among several, with war disruption and deficit financing the larger drivers of that year's GDP and inflation.
An amendment to an existing budget act, a narrower and more procedural instrument than a full annual budget law.
A budget amendment is a minor procedural adjustment against a GDP collapse driven mainly by war onset, capital flight and frozen assets.
A routine wartime annual budget law, one of many instruments in the Government Finance category.
A wartime budget is one force among several, with Tanker War disruption to oil exports the larger driver of that year's GDP and inflation.
A routine wartime annual budget law, one of many instruments in the Government Finance category.
A wartime budget is one force among several, with the war's peak destruction and oil-revenue swings the larger driver of that year's GDP and inflation.
This act created the planning bureaucracy that produces budget and GDP data for decades, but it is an institutional-creation law, not itself a fiscal or output policy instrument.
The organization this law created is the administrative author of the annual budget bills behind these totals charts, a real but institutional rather than direct fiscal channel.
An institution-founding law is too indirect a channel to expect a visible effect on fixed capital formation.
A routine annual budget bill submitted amid an oil-price collapse, one of many instruments in the Government Finance category.
The budget is a secondary factor next to the 1986 oil-price collapse and peak war spending that principally drove that year's GDP and inflation.
A narrow administrative fee-schedule amendment confined to free-trade zones, tangential to national customs duty revenue.
A narrow decree adding a handful of provincial revenue line items, a small addition to the public revenue base.
A narrow directive licensing energy service companies, a marginal administrative channel against national energy-use and intensity trends.
Fuel-quality and emission standards are a real, direct lever on urban PM2.5 exposure, but one force among several including traffic growth and industry.
A vehicle fuel-emissions bylaw has no real channel to household access to clean cooking fuels.
A routine clause addition to an implementing bylaw, procedural machinery with no material trade effect expected.
A narrow court ruling interpreting one article of a decades-old bylaw, a procedural legal interpretation rather than a land-reform policy instrument.
A narrow 2002 interpretive court ruling, not the underlying land reform program itself, so its expected reach into GDP is negligible.
A court ruling on insurance entitlement for a subset of laid-off workers does not affect the measured unemployment rate.
Directly concerns unemployment-insurance payout eligibility within the Social Security Organization, a real but marginal effect on aggregate pensioners and amounts paid.
A single agricultural bank's bond authorization is negligible against the aggregate stock of government liabilities.
A narrow credit-expansion authorization for one state bank, marginal to rural development broadly.
A single bond tranche authorized under one budget clause, a real but small addition to that year's debt and budget totals.
The subsidy reform this bylaw implements reset administered energy and bread prices nationwide, driving a documented, dateable jump in inflation.
The reform raised fuel prices toward international levels, a major instrument with a material, visible expected effect on domestic energy consumption.
Cash transfers funded by subsidy removal changed household disposable income, a real but partial and indirect effect on consumption expenditure.
The VAT Act is the explicit legal instrument creating and governing the tax that is the series it targets.
A broad consumption tax produces a real but one-off, partial pass-through effect on the price level, marginal against overall CPI dynamics.
A circular that only communicates existing budget-law duty provisions to customs offices, an implementation notice rather than a new policy lever.
Amends duty and charge collection rules for importers, a real but partial regulatory channel to import-related revenue.
The foundational statute governing income, profit and property taxes since 1988, the law that defines direct tax revenue.
The national tax code shapes economy-wide fiscal capacity and investment incentives, a real but partial force among many on GDP.
An omnibus financing-facilitation law for industrial modernization, a real but partial force among many shaping sectoral value added.
One implementing provision of a development plan is too narrow and indirect to expect a visible effect on aggregate GDP.
The foundational national statute governing all licensing and customs procedure across Iran's entire trade regime.
The trade-regime statute is a small force against trade-to-GDP growth ratios, which are driven mainly by oil prices and exchange rates.
This FY1386 budget law is the direct legal instrument setting the totals shown in the Annual Budget Law Totals chart.
The annual budget law directly sets the government spending envelope that this consumption-expenditure series measures.
A duplicate entry of the foundational Export/Import Regulations Act, the core statutory framework for all Iranian trade.
A duplicate entry; the trade-regime statute is a small force against trade-to-GDP growth ratios driven mainly by oil prices and exchange rates.
A duplicate entry of the Direct Taxation Act, the foundational statute that defines direct tax revenue.
A duplicate entry; the national tax code is a real but partial force among many shaping GDP.
This bylaw enabled privatized state enterprises, which were the source of a large share of new stock-market listings and trading volume in this period.
An implementing bylaw for enterprise classification, a narrower administrative mechanism than the headline Article 44 privatization law, with only a partial effect on GDP.
Governs the restructuring and sale of state industrial and mining enterprises, a real but partial effect on sectoral value-added composition.
An omnibus procedural amendment to state financial-management rules, general but real background to Government Finance.
Adjusts state-company oversight and budget-execution rules, only marginally touching the scale of government consumption expenditure.
A direct precursor to gasoline rationing that targeted fuel consumption, a real but partial force alongside the larger 2010 subsidy price reform.
Mandates transit investment as a substitute for private-vehicle fuel use, a real but partial channel to transport infrastructure.
A narrow rangeland and fodder-supply fund, a marginal input-supply channel to livestock output.
This FY1383 budget law is the direct legal instrument setting the totals shown in the Annual Budget Law Totals chart.
A routine annual budget law, one of many instruments in the Government Finance category.
A 1940 supplementary budget act that modifies spending line items rather than macro aggregates, and predates the chart series.
This act is the direct legal authorization for a specific World Bank loan, the exact instrument this borrowing-history series records.
